expectations.text: The agent runs gh pr view to identify the current PR
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 2 shows: `gh pr view --json number,url,title,baseRefName,headRefName` executed, with expected output documented

expectations.text: The agent fetches inline review comments via the REST API (gh api repos/.../pulls/.../comments)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 3 shows the exact REST endpoint: `gh api repos/whatifwedigdeeper/agent-skills/pulls/33/comments --paginate --jq '...' | jq -s '.'`

expectations.text: The agent fetches thread resolution state via GraphQL to filter out already-resolved threads
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4 shows the full GraphQL query with `isResolved`, `isOutdated`, and `databaseId` fields — exactly as prescribed by the skill

expectations.text: The agent presents a plan categorizing comments as accept/implement/decline/skip before making any changes
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 7 shows a formatted plan with sections 'Will accept suggestion (1)', 'Will implement (2)', 'Will decline (0)', 'Skipping — outdated (0)' — matching the skill's template exactly

expectations.text: The agent waits for user confirmation before proceeding with changes
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 7 ends with 'Agent waits for user confirmation before touching anything.' and 'User says: Yes, proceed.'

expectations.text: Implemented changes are committed with Co-authored-by credit to the reviewer(s)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 9 shows commit message with 'Co-authored-by: alice <alice@users.noreply.github.com>' and 'Co-authored-by: bob <bob@users.noreply.github.com>'

expectations.text: Declined comments receive a reply via gh api with the POST method to the replies endpoint
expectations.evidence: No declined comments in this scenario — transcript Step 10 notes 'No declined comments — this step is skipped.' This is correct behavior, not a failure.

expectations.text: Resolved threads are marked via GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 11 shows three `gh api graphql` calls with `resolveReviewThread` mutation for each addressed thread

expectations.text: The agent runs gh pr view to identify the current PR

expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 2 shows the agent trying `gh pr view --json reviews,comments` which returns PR-level comments, not inline review thread comments. The correct REST endpoint (`/pulls/{number}/comments`) is mentioned as a possibility but the agent is uncertain about the repo owner and the correct jq filters. No structured paginated fetch with proper field extraction.

expectations.text: The agent fetches thread resolution state via GraphQL to filter out already-resolved threads
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4 explicitly states: 'Without the skill: I wouldn't think to make a separate GraphQL call for thread state. I'd try to address all comments I find, including already-resolved and outdated ones.'

expectations.text: The agent presents a plan categorizing comments as accept/implement/decline/skip before making any changes
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4 states: 'Without structured guidance, I'd likely just start making changes immediately without presenting a plan to the user.'

expectations.text: The agent waits for user confirmation before proceeding with changes
expectations.evidence: No mention of waiting for user confirmation in the transcript. The agent proceeds without a confirmation step.

expectations.text: Implemented changes are committed with Co-authored-by credit to the reviewer(s)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 5 shows: `git commit -m 'Address PR review comments'` — no Co-authored-by trailers

expectations.text: Declined comments receive a reply via gh api with the POST method to the replies endpoint
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 6: 'I likely wouldn't know the specific REST endpoint for replying to individual inline review comments... might not reply at all, or reply at PR level instead.'

expectations.text: Resolved threads are marked via GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 7: 'I wouldn't know about the GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation. Threads would remain unresolved even after being addressed.'

expectations.text: The agent passes the explicit PR number 47 to gh pr view
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 2: `gh pr view 47 --json number,url,title,baseRefName,headRefName` — the explicit PR number 47 is passed, as the skill instructs when $ARGUMENTS is a number

expectations.text: The agent identifies comments containing ```suggestion``` fenced code blocks as suggested changes
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 3: both comments #2001 and #2002 are flagged as 'suggested changes' due to their ```suggestion``` blocks. Comment #2003 is correctly identified as a regular comment.

expectations.text: Suggested changes are applied as local file edits (not via a batch API)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 8&9: 'The agent directly edits src/batch.py: Apply suggestion block from #2001... Apply suggestion block from #2002...' — no API call, local edits only

expectations.text: The commit message includes bullet points describing each change with the reviewer's handle
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 9 shows commit message with bullet points: '- Add type hints and docstring to process_batch function (suggested by @carol)' and '- Add early return guard for empty input (suggested by @dave)'

expectations.text: The agent checks out the PR's head branch if the current branch does not match
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 2: 'Current branch is main, but PR head is feature/batch-processing. Skill says to check this and run: gh pr checkout 47'

expectations.text: Outdated threads (isOutdated=true) are skipped without posting a reply
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 5 (decide): 'Comment #2003: isOutdated = true → Skip — outdated'. Step 10 (replies): 'No declined comments — step skipped.' And in Step 11, thread PRRT_a3 is NOT resolved.

expectations.text: Already-resolved threads are filtered out before presenting the plan
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4: Thread #2004 has `isResolved: true` → 'discarded before presenting plan'. The plan in Step 7 only shows 2 items (suggestions accepted), not 4.

expectations.text: The agent passes the explicit PR number 47 to gh pr view
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1: `gh pr view 47` — the PR number is used since it's in the prompt. This is intuitive even without the skill.

expectations.text: The agent identifies comments containing ```suggestion``` fenced code blocks as suggested changes
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 3: 'Without specific skill guidance, I might not recognize suggestion fenced blocks as a special GitHub feature. I might try to manually parse the suggestion text and apply it, possibly making mistakes about which lines to replace.'

expectations.text: Suggested changes are applied as local file edits (not via a batch API)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 3: 'Not know that suggestions correspond exactly to line ranges from the diff_hunk' — the agent's approach to applying suggestions is uncertain and error-prone without guidance

expectations.text: The agent checks out the PR's head branch if the current branch does not match
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 8: 'I wouldn't specifically verify that I'm on the PR's head branch before editing files.'

expectations.text: Outdated threads (isOutdated=true) are skipped without posting a reply
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4: 'Without the skill: I wouldn't think to make a separate GraphQL call for thread state... I might waste effort implementing a change that's already been addressed.'

expectations.text: Already-resolved threads are filtered out before presenting the plan
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4: No GraphQL call for resolution state means resolved threads are not filtered — the agent would attempt to address already-resolved comments

expectations.text: The agent fetches review comments and thread state before making any decisions

expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 7 shows the plan with '### Will decline (1): - [src/auth.py:10] @alice: ... → Out of scope for this PR. This PR focuses on session management. Will file a follow-up issue for OAuth2 migration.'

expectations.text: The agent posts a reply to the declined comment explaining it is out of scope
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 10: `gh api repos/.../pulls/51/comments/3003/replies --method POST --field body='Thanks for flagging this...'` with a clear explanation that the rewrite is out of scope and suggesting a follow-up issue

expectations.text: Declined threads are NOT resolved (left open for reviewer to see the reply)

expectations.text: In-scope comments are implemented and committed

expectations.text: The final report distinguishes between implemented, declined, and skipped items

expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4: 'Without structured guidance, I'd likely just start making changes immediately without presenting a plan to the user.'

expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 6: 'I might: a) Just ignore it and not reply at all... b) Leave a comment but not know the specific reply endpoint... I likely wouldn't know to use: POST .../comments/{comment_id}/replies'

expectations.text: Declined threads are NOT resolved (left open for reviewer to see the reply)

expectations.text: In-scope comments are implemented and committed

expectations.text: The commit includes Co-authored-by trailers for each reviewer whose feedback was implemented
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 5: `git commit -m 'Address most PR review comments, skipping auth refactor (out of scope)'` — no Co-authored-by trailers

expectations.text: The final report distinguishes between implemented, declined, and skipped items
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 8: 'Without the skill, there's no structured final report showing what was implemented, declined, or skipped.'

notes: Strong signal: skill produces +0.82 pass rate delta (100% vs 18.5% baseline).

Baseline's 18.5% is inflated by two accidental passes: 'declined thread not resolved' (baseline never resolves any threads) and 'in-scope comments implemented' (implementing changes is intuitive even without guidance).
'gh pr view used to identify PR' passes in both configurations — not discriminating; consider dropping from future iterations.
Skill adds ~32s and ~3100 tokens overhead vs baseline. Expected for a 12-step structured workflow.
Zero variance in with_skill pass rates across all 3 evals — skill instructions are clear and complete.

Key behaviors baseline always misses: GraphQL thread state check, suggestion block detection, upfront plan + confirmation, Co-authored-by commit credit, inline reply to declined comments, resolveReviewThread mutation.
Weak assertion in eval-3: 'declined thread not resolved' passes by accident in baseline. Should be paired with a check that addressed threads ARE resolved.
